name: Golden Bubble Ring
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 2 1/2 cups of flour and the yeast.
3. Heat milk, 1/2 cup sugar, shortening, and 1 teaspoon salt in a saucepan just until warm (115-120°F), stirring constantly until shortening almost melts.
4. Add the warm mixture to the dry ingredients; add eggs.
5. Beat at low speed of an electric mixer for 1/2 minute, scraping the bowl.
6. Beat at high speed for 3 minutes.
7. Stir in enough remaining flour by hand to form a soft dough.
8. Knead on a lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 8 to 10 minutes.
9. Form the dough into a ball and place in a greased bowl. Turn once. Cover; let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 to 1 1/4 hours.
10. Grease a 10-inch tube pan.
11. Shape the dough into 28 balls. Roll each in melted butter, then in a mixture of 3/4 cup sugar and cinnamon.
12. Arrange the balls in the pan and sprinkle with any remaining sugar-cinnamon mixture. If nuts are used, scatter among the balls as they are placed in the pan.
13. Let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
14. Bake at 350°F for 35 to 40 minutes.
15. Cool in the pan for 15 to 20 minutes. Invert onto a rack and remove the pan.
